creator: cherry@trombik.org
text:
Sorry if my PR sounds offending.

On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 09:32:22PM -0500, Paul Schmehl wrote:
> 
> That is correct.  I was asked to fix the security/bro port because it was 
> out of date.  Since doing that, I have discovered that bro is much more 
> complex than my port allows for.  So, I began working on 
> security/bro-devel, but Chris Lee, who was working with me on some 
> patches, volunteered to take it over.  I don't know what, if anything, he 
> has done with the port since.

All I need is a working port; respecting user's choice, better current
practice, common standard in ports tree. If his port is available, fine.
The development branch (I'm not talking about svn tree) is "stable"
since the last release, 2007, Jul. If he has made any progress, I'd
really love to know.

> The port requires someone who not only uses 
> bro and understands its intricacies but can also deal with source code 
> patching to get the software to work on FreeBSD.

Additionally, some feedbacks to the upstream will make it better. The
port shouldn't have many patches. The upstream is developing on FreeBSD.

-- 
Tomoyuki Sakurai
